steps.title: Use the password evidence
steps.content: "Nonetheless" contrasts expert advice to make complicated passwords with what users actually do. The example of "123456789" being the third most commonly used password shows a widespread preference for convenience.

steps.title: Reject mismatched meanings
steps.content: A does not fit because the example is not inventive. B is too vague; the evidence is about common use, not usefulness. C contradicts the easy-to-remember example and the contrast with complicated passwords. The answer is D.
